title: Traveling with Kyle - Peru Part 3/7 - Nazca to Cuzco

transcript.text: Was uh so this video okay yeah so I fly I take the bus into kuzco now the ride from nazco to
Kuzco up the mountain was a beautiful beautiful beautiful trip it would have been an amazing bike
Ride yet I was too lazy or something to not to do it well um yeah just switch back switchbacks
Up up up up up up up and then down and up up up up up up up climbing would have been amazing
Um and then we go way up over this thing and then we go down into kuzco and a valley
Kuzco the elevation of kuzco I think 7 000 feet so it's high it's kind of like the same as like
Mexico city so very temperate nice and cool even though it's kind of close to the equator I guess
I bet it's a beautiful city um kind of big too maybe the size of boulder I would say
Um you know five miles across or something um yeah so it was very exciting for me to go there
Because that's where the incas come from um got off the bus rode into the main plaza and then got a
Hotel up near there and um and I saw my friend dan the uh israeli who I saw in the hotel in the hostel
In kuzco I mean in ikido and then in huraz I saw him and then there and uh he was staying at the
Same hotel I was and I was going to do the inca trail next day so I jumped and did the uh tour with
Him and his uh german girlfriend and you know on the bus and there was this other english guy and
A couple american guys and let's see english mother and daughter team and I guess who else
So we drove over there and we get off and um um we uh hike in the first day and uh inca trail is
Beautiful it's all paved cobblestone roads we did the four day I guess you can elect to do the half
Where you kind of only hike a little bit and then you go in on the on the river but we did the four
Day where we go in the river and then we stayed we were camping out in tents that uh they gave us to
Carry but I guess you could opt to have somebody else carry your stuff and uh yeah we hiked we
Camped one day next to the thing and I think they were cooking for us so that was cool it was fun
And uh we had like a peruvian female guide and then like a couple of sherpas would carry the food
And uh and then we camped the next day and there was another ruin there's like lots of ruins on the
Way there I guess uh the uh the king would would be able to go to um machu picchu along the river
But the normal people had to take the inca trail kind of like some kind of rise of passage or something
And uh yeah so we camped the second night and then it was over the pass and the pass was like 13 000
Feet or something big high high high trip now the inca trail is I mean we were we were a pack of
You know well what 15 people in our pack but there were like tons of packs all over the place so
Like we'd we'd stop and have the tour and the person would explain and there'd be like somebody
Else's pack there so kind of we would like intermingle and talk to the other packs but there
Were packs and packs and packs and packs like maybe we had like sister packs that we'd be like
Camping out together whatever but but I mean there's hundreds of people on the inca trail at
All times just back thousands of people um so yeah it was interesting going over the pass because
It was just jam-packed literally bumper to bumper traffic all the way up with tourists
Which I thought was cool I mean maybe a lot of people were like but I thought it was kind of
Cool there were so many people there and I like powered up there and passing people all the way up
I was the first person in my group to the top so I was hanging out up there and I I bought a bunch of
Uh coca leaves like in kuska or something so I was like in there stuffing my face both sides like a
Chipmunk trying to get as high as I possibly could and I think I was getting a buzz on
Um that's what all of the uh nepalese I mean the uh peruvians do is they eat it for fun and that
Stuff that plant's actually illegal there nice going fda um and yeah peruvians williams aren't
Very happy about that by the way um but uh I get plant illegally fucking kidding me anyways um
So I'm waiting on the top of the pass for like a couple hours for the rest of my pack to get up
There and then you go down the pass and that and and on the other side it was totally paved it was
Like uh 20 feet 15 feet wide of totally paved stairs then this is like hundreds of years old
And I guess they uncovered it um you know just maybe 100 years ago because they actually I think
Maybe they hit it and stuff they didn't want the spanish to come and destroy it because when the
Spanish came they destroyed all the shit that they could anyways um so they uh we hiked down there
And then count in the bottom of the little river there and and then hiked up again there's another
Ruin and then we went over this little pass on the other side there's like a mini machu picchu
There's like this castle basically built on the top of a ridge um that's really steep goes down you
Know if you go on the edge it's like down down down down down and that's a nice you know open grass
Areas it would have been just and it beautiful to live amazingly beautiful live great tranquil place
And I guess they would you know do their spiritual practice or whatever they did and then and I mean
They had some great water irrigation because it's all up top of a ridge so they had uh you could
Actually see this uh stone water you know man-made like little river feeding it from up above and
Then coming down in and passing through all of the you know rooms and stuff it's pretty amazing
And then we uh continued on the paved trail and camped out another time and then and then we went
Over this bridge it was kind of like over a ridge and this paved path overlooking on both sides very
Beautiful and then we could actually see the terraces uh because to feed themselves they
Actually made terraces near machu picchu a couple of them very big lots huge huge huge um for off in
The distance and uh so we hiked down there and camped out um and there was actually a restaurant
There and it was funny there there's there's some guy did some kind of big toast for all of his group
And somebody tried to get me to do one for mine I didn't do it I should have them but uh and then the
Next day it was like going to machu picchu and uh we weren't that we had breakfast and we weren't the
First group to leave and so there's lots of people in front of me and I'm hiking it was like
More bumper to bumper packed then my buddy dan came hiking charging through just passing people I guess
You know europeans have different uh different spatial requirements customs so I kind of got on his
Butt and like followed him up and then passing people and then when it got like sparse enough I started
Running and uh there's this like one area where you go over a pass and then you can actually see
Machu picchu and there's like this keyhole or something there's this like gate to go in and
I was watching that a little and then I ran down so I was the first person to get to machu picchu that
Morning and I was hanging out and I walked around it and then uh our uh our group had us do a tour um
We had a tour guide this little peruvian guy who was like really like grumpy and angry and stuff like
Didn't take no shit but he was a really good tour guide and he explained everything like took his
Stuff really seriously but he was a great tour guide and uh yeah it was interesting I guess machu picchu
Was still being built when the spanish came because up on the very top of it they had like a big stone in
The middle of some uh you know plaza type area that was in the middle and he said yeah they were in the
Process of dragging the stone across you know building the wall and getting it more until when
They got word the spanish had come and they just abandoned it because they didn't want the spanish
To find machu picchu and you know dismantle everything so that's when it overgrew and the
Spanish never found it until hiram bingham found it in like 1915 or whatever but uh yeah I I did the
Hike up um after I guess is too we did the hike up to the top of that um big mountain now I didn't know
And I looked when you look at photos of machu picchu in the background you see this really steep like tower
Well you can hike to the top of that there's a trail all the way to the top of that the incas
Made it all paved and everything and now this is on the on the edge of like super sheer cliff so like
You you know you got to kind of stay a little bit farther away from the edge uh you know um
And um and even at the very top there's it gets really steep these stairs that are like this
To get up to this like building number I don't know how they built it those people did not have vertigo
There's an actual building up there and then you could go even to the top and there are these
Stones these huge stones you can sit on top of them you know on top of everything we're looking
Everything it's amazing and um yeah so then I came down and we looked at machu picchu and I was I mean
There was like this little cave even in there that like looked like a condor it was called condor cave
Because they even like put some stones in there make it look like it there's lots of mountains called
Condor and condorini and stuff in south america that's like their national bird um but uh yeah
Machu picchu is amazing we saw the little uh apartment buildings they had there where they
Lived and um their central uh grass area and it's like right up on the top of a ridge really steep ridge
On the top of mountains amazing uh with a big river down below and on one side you know you can see the
River way way down below like on the edge of a cliff on the eastern side but um uh I got some
Serious energy out of that I mean big big it's an energy place definitely like tkau um you know
And uh so yeah then we hiked back down into like some like little town on the river and then took
The train back um to kuzco whoopsies so I guess I'll continue there
